abstract.para: For &sle; &productnumber;, the System Python is Python 3.13.

section.title: Python interpreter lifecycles
section.para: &sle; &productnumber; includes several Python interpreter versions with different support lifecycles:

listitem.para: System Python is the Python version for which &suse; provides a large set of Python modules needed for &sle; &productnumber; applications and solutions.
The System Python version and its modules remain stable and are supported throughout the &sle; &productnumber; lifecycle.
Support covers both the General Support and Long Term Support (LTS) periods of &slsa; &productnumber; and applies for each future &sle; 16.x minor release.

Developer Python provides a more recent Python version. Unlike System Python, it is intended to be used together with Python wheels provided by PyPI, which are installed and maintained via pip and pipx.
It is supported during the General Support period of &slsa; &productnumber; but excludes LTS.
In future &sle; 16.x minor releases, System Python will be updated to a newer version. At that time, the previous System Python version and all its modules will be provided as legacy Python.

section.title: Python release cycle
section.para: &sle; &productnumber; follows a defined release and support schedule for Python interpreter versions.
In &sle; &productnumber;, the System Python is Python 3.13. The current plan (subject to change) is to provide newer Python versions in future &sle; 16.x releases.
A Developer Python version will be released with each minor release and will be supported only for the general support period of that release (two years).
